title: Native-reader shaping & correctness review for the community script font
context: For any under-served script, the Elyos quality bar requires native-reader / script-authority correctness review before release. After glyphs and shaping are drawn (fonts-script-202), one or more native readers or script authorities must review the rendered output for correctness and cultural appropriateness. A font with wrong script forms is worse than no font; this review is a hard gate, not optional.
objective: Coordinate and document native-reader and script-authority correctness review of the drawn script font, covering glyph forms, contextual shaping, diacritics, spacing, and cultural appropriateness; record 100% pass or remediation steps before release.
acceptanceCriteria: At least one native reader or script authority provides a documented correctness review of the rendered output against community-standard forms.
Review covers: glyph forms, contextual shaping (GSUB/GPOS), diacritic placement, spacing, and cultural/community appropriateness.
Any errors identified result in changes to fonts-script-202 before the release task (fonts-script-204) proceeds; all identified errors are resolved or explicitly acknowledged as known limitations.
Native-reader correctness review is recorded as passing (100%) or the unresolved issues are documented with community agreement.
Reviewer credit is recorded only with explicit consent; pseudonyms honored; no personal data published without consent.
Review report is CC-BY-4.0; no identifiable personal data included without consent.
resources: PLAN.md (section 8: required reviews — script authority sign-off)
fonts-script-201 (orthography and design brief — defines the target forms)
output: A native-reader correctness review report (CC-BY-4.0) documenting sign-off (or remediation steps) on glyph forms, shaping, and cultural appropriateness, merged into the repository before the M2 release task.
